currentTask.name: Git workflow overhaul — enable branch protection, CI pipeline, and fix smoke tests
currentTask.goal: Audit and fix all git workflow gaps identified in the initial scan

whatChanged: scripts/git-agent.sh: removed --no-verify from 4 commit paths (kept in WIP trap)
.github/workflows/ci.yml: created with shellcheck, secrets scan, smoke tests, Playwright
rules/common/git-workflow.md: documented two-tier commit system (checkpoint English + PR conventional)
scripts/checkpoint-commit.sh: added conventional commit advisory linting
scripts/test-smoke.sh: fixed 3 pre-existing failures (bwrap skip, tool_count 132→134, broken pipe)
verification: Branch protection active on main: PR required, strict checks [Lint & Validate, Smoke Tests], linear history, no force-push, enforce admins
CI fully green: 115/115 smoke tests pass, Lint & Validate passes
Global pre-commit hook (identity gate) confirmed passing
Only WIP crash trap retains --no-verify (confirmed via grep)
keyLearnings: tools.sh --json output is large — piping through echo+grep causes SIGPIPE. Fixed by pre-extracting with python.
npm install -g doesn't help node -e require() — must install locally for browser.sh to work in CI.
gh api --field stringifies objects. Must pipe JSON body via --input - for nested structures.
Branch protection applies server-side only — local commits are unaffected until push.
Smoke test expectations (tool_count) drift when tools are added — needs regular updating.
residualRisk: branch protection prevents direct push to main. Agent sessions must use end --pr instead of end --merge. The WIP trap in git-agent.sh still uses --no-verify (intentional, crash safety net).
